texto: Thinking of possibilities is like driving a car on a freeway. You have an open road that stretches endlessly before you where your thoughts are not shackled. But when we say 'impossible,' we have already reached a dead-end in our minds. So dwell on possibilities to open up your horizon.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pankaj Patel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Thinking of possibilities is like driving a car on a freeway. You have an open road that stretche…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, road, thinking pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e viagem.

frase_original: Thinking of possibilities is like driving a car on a freeway. You have an open road that stretches endlessly before you where your thoughts are not shackled. But when we say 'impossible,' we have already reached a dead-end in our minds. So dwell on possibilities to open up your horizon.

texto: One of the big myths about philanthropy is that it's all about donating funds for a cause. I like to look at it quite differently. Philanthropy is about 'giving' - not just in monetary terms but also in non-monetary aspects, like time, ideas, or being a volunteer. Donating money is just a small part of philanthropy.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pankaj Patel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “One of the big myths about philanthropy is that it's all about donating funds for a cause. I like…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, time, giving pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de giving e small.
